title: Episode 23 NPC home video version
wikitext:
[]: Episode 23 NPC/home video version             Quote Post by meghatas » Tue Jun 18, 2024 4:54 pm Hiya, I've been searching far and wide to be able to watch the NPC/home video release (I know that I might be butchering these terms, as I belive there are a thousand home video versions) of episode 23, to be able to watch the version with the "mysterious images" over the tank scene. Not sure if I am even allowed to ask something like this, but.. is there any way to watch this version that anyone knows of on this forum? I've literally been looking for it (on and off) for years now, and it is maddening. Thanks!

[]: Re: Episode 23 NPC/home video version             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Jun 19, 2024 5:56 am If I might suggest a legal means, I can confirm (since I own it) that this Japanese dvd contains said version: https://svenge.evamonkey.com/ Also, if you happen to own the Bluray collection (English or Japanese), the extended intro contains most of the mysterious images with better visibility. I can't find a Youtube link for the scene from the episode, but this article on the wiki links to a Youtube video where the scene is filtered so you can see the mysterious images better. It also provides a thorough frame by frame analysis.

[]: Re: Episode 23 NPC/home video version             Quote Post by meghatas » Tue Jun 25, 2024 4:48 pm Thanks Axx°N N. I am aware of the Wikipedia page, I've been re-reading it over and over again since I first stumbled upon it, that is what has mainly sparked my interest. Could you perhaps be more specific about which DVD you are referring to from the site? They offer many variations. Thanks! However, as I reside in Hungary, I am afraid that procuring the DVD might not be without challenges. Would you know of any page where even just the scene itself including the 'mysterious images' could be seen by chance? Thanks!

[]: Re: Episode 23 NPC/home video version             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Jun 26, 2024 4:51 am meghatas wrote: Could you perhaps be more specific about which DVD you are referring to from the site? They offer many variations. Thanks!
